summary: Iteration 14 — Backend-only verification of 3 new feature sets: (1) WebRTC calling signaling /api/calls/*, (2) Global search /api/search, (3) Admin Project Finance & Invoicing /api/finance/*. Created /app/backend/tests/test_iter14_calls_search_finance.py with 49 tests across 8 classes (TestCalling, TestSearch, TestFinanceCRUD, TestFinancePayments, TestFinanceEmails, TestFinanceFolders, TestFinanceInvoices, TestFinanceCleanup). 49/49 new tests PASS. Full regression: 251/251 pytest tests pass (202 prior + 49 new). Gmail OAuth is intentionally NOT connected — POST /api/finance/invoices/{id}/send and POST /api/finance/projects/{id}/reminder both return clean 400 with 'Gmail not connected' message (no 5xx). All finance responses scrub Mongo _id, exclude heavy content_base64 in list/get, and exclude pdf_base64 in invoice list/get while serving the PDF binary via /pdf endpoint with %PDF magic bytes & application/pdf content-type. Auto-incremented invoice_no format verified as PRX-YYYY-NNNNN. Payment-by-percent (locked_budget * percent/100) verified. First client_email auto-promoted to primary; setting a new primary unsets others; deleting the primary re-promotes a remaining email. Folder/file upload, 10MB limit (400), and base64 download verified. Stub project auto-created in db.projects when finance is added without project_id (visible in /api/projects). Duplicate finance for same project_id correctly rejected with 400. Calls: cannot-self (400), missing-callee (404), incoming polling, callee answer flips ringing→active with SDP stored, already-answered (400), non-callee answer (403), ICE candidates strictly return OTHER party's candidates only, stranger access (403), end/decline auth. Search: returns grouped dict with no _id leak; empty q returns total:0; case-insensitive; finance + invoices groups appear when matches exist.

minor.issue: When amount=0 and percent provided but locked_budget is 0/missing, response is 400 — correct, but the message ('Provide a positive amount or a percent of a locked budget') doesn't distinguish missing-locked-budget vs missing-percent. Cosmetic.

If Gmail were connected and a downstream Gmail/HTTP exception happened, the handler wraps it as HTTPException(502, 'Send failed: <raw exception>')—may leak provider error details. Consider sanitising. Not exercised in this run (Gmail off path returns 400 cleanly, as required).
When pdf_base64 is missing the handler rebuilds the PDF inline — fine, but it bypasses the urllib timeout check failure path silently (logo fetch is wrapped). Acceptable.

Both module-load with BASE = os.environ['REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL'].rstrip('/'); they crash collection in environments where the variable isn't exported. Workaround: set env before pytest. Recommend converting to the safer pattern used in other test files that fall back to reading /app/frontend/.env.

verification_results.regression: 251/251 tests pass across full backend/tests suite (202 prior + 49 new).

action_items: Consider converting test_phase3.py and test_issues_and_cert.py to the resilient env-loading pattern used by test_notif_email.py / test_iter14_*.py (fallback to /app/frontend/.env) so they don't fail at pytest collection when REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL isn't exported in the shell.
Optional: sanitize the 502 'Send failed: <raw>' string from finance.send_invoice / send_reminder to avoid leaking provider exception details when Gmail is connected.
Optional: clarify 400 message when payment percent is provided but locked_budget is missing.
critical_code_review_comments: finance.py is 1003 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into finance_routes.py + finance_pdf.py + finance_invoices.py for maintainability.
search_module.py runs ~10 sequential Mongo finds per request; for larger workspaces consider asyncio.gather to parallelize the regex finds.

_build_invoice_pdf does a synchronous urllib.request.urlopen to fetch the Projexino logo (timeout=4) inside an async endpoint — blocks the event loop. Move into a thread executor (asyncio.to_thread / fastapi BackgroundTask) or cache the logo bytes at startup.
calling.py: /calls/incoming uses ISO-string cutoff comparison ($gte) — works because ISO strings sort chronologically, but storing created_at as a real datetime would be more robust and indexable.
calling.py: ICE candidates have no retention cleanup — db.call_ice will grow unboundedly. Add a TTL index on call_ice.at or purge on call end.

finance.py: POST /finance/projects/{id}/folders/{fid}/files validates 'size' from client but doesn't verify the decoded base64 length matches/within MAX_FILE_BYTES — a client could lie about size to bypass the limit. Decode and len-check the bytes server-side.
All finance responses correctly scrub _id via _scrub helper or projection — verified no leaks. Good.
send_invoice and send_reminder embed user-provided extra_message/message via f-string into HTML email without sanitization — XSS risk in the rendered email. Use html.escape() before embedding.
seed_data_creation: Created and then deleted a TEST_FIN_<hex> finance project (with its auto-stub projects row left in place — admin can manually clean it from /api/projects if desired). One invoice was generated under it and remains in db.invoices after finance deletion (intentional — invoices outlive the finance doc).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 new feature areas (calling/search/finance) are green. The TEST_FIN_* finance project is deleted at end of run; orphaned stub project + 1 issued invoice may remain — safe to ignore. If main agent fixes any of the action items (esp. PDF logo blocking I/O or base64 size guard), retest just the affected class with `pytest backend/tests/test_iter14_calls_search_finance.py::TestFinanceFolders -v`. The two legacy test files (test_phase3, test_issues_and_cert) need REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exported in shell or they fail at collection; not a backend regression.
rca_of_the_issue: n/a — no bugs found in new code paths.
